page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 524 GB temporary disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU execution, multi-threaded decompression, and ray tracing. Memory performance is highly polarized: cached small-block read operations rank in the top 25%, whereas uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average tier. Single-core processing, software compilation, and database operations also demonstrate average performance. Cryptographic execution and Redis SET operations are highly efficient, registering in the top 25% of benchmarked systems.

From a resource density perspective, the D64as_v4 offers a balanced hardware profile without the added cost of dedicated GPU hardware. Tradeoffs include average single-threaded performance and moderate uncached memory bandwidth, which may limit performance in memory-bound sequential workloads. However, the high core count and strong multi-threaded execution make this server appropriate for multi-threaded data processing, Redis caching layers, image processing, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 x86_64 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores), 256.0 GB of RAM, and a 524 GB local storage drive with Premium Storage capability. Operating at 3.525 GHz, the server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, cached memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution, though its single-core speeds and uncached memory bandwidth remain average. This balanced resource allocation provides qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads without the premium of specialized hardware. It is suited for Redis deployments, multi-threaded decompression, image processing, and moderate LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local storage for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs operating at 3.525 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and Redis SET performance
Average single-core execution and uncached memory bandwidth
Suited for multi-threaded data processing, Redis caching, and image processing
